title: Cyclosorus skinneri
intro: Cyclosorus skinneri, also known as Skins Fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Blechnaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are said to aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus skinneri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in some traditional remedies for its ability to treat fever, cough, and other respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus skinneri is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a part of the order Polypodiales in the class Polypodiopsida. This classification places Cyclosorus skinneri within the larger group of ferns. The species is also known to be a member of the genus Cyclosorus, which is a group of ferns that are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. The exact classification of Cyclosorus skinneri is further divided into the species level, where it is recognized as a distinct species within the genus. At the highest taxonomic level, Cyclosorus skinneri is a member of the kingdom Plantae.
